name: Parry
description: A precisely timed defensive action that turns an incoming attack into advantage.
design_notes: Parry depends on readable enemy attacks and consistent timing windows. It should define what can be parried, what reward is granted, and how failure is punished. Strong parry design avoids hidden exceptions: if grabs, projectiles, heavy attacks, or rear hits cannot be parried, the player needs signals before impact.
design_purpose: Reward players for reading attack timing rather than only avoiding contact.
Create a high-skill defensive option with a distinct risk profile from block or dodge.
Open counterattack, stagger, guard break, or resource rewards after a successful read.
player_fantasy: The player feels composed under pressure, turning enemy aggression into a controlled counter moment.

parameters.name: parry_startup_ms
parameters.description: Delay between input and the first active parry frame.

parameters.name: parry_active_window_ms
parameters.description: Window in which eligible incoming attacks are deflected.

parameters.name: parry_recovery_ms
parameters.description: Post-attempt recovery before full control returns.

parameters.name: attacker_stagger_frames
parameters.description: Stagger applied to the attacker after a successful parry.

edge_cases: Multi-hit attacks must specify whether one parry covers all hits or only the first strike.
Projectile ownership should transfer or be destroyed without damaging the parrying player later.
Parry during rollback, pause, hit stop, or slow motion must use one timing clock.
Attacks from behind, above, or outside a guard arc need explicit eligibility metadata.
common_bugs: The active window is tied to animation frames and changes when animation playback rate changes.
Both parry success and damage are applied because hit resolution order is inconsistent.
Parry effects trigger on friendly or environmental hits that should be ignored.
Network prediction shows parry success locally but server authority still applies damage.
balancing_notes: A generous active window can make parry replace block and dodge unless failure recovery or eligibility limits matter.
Rewards should match risk: safe parries need modest payoff, while strict parries can justify large stagger or counter windows.
accessibility_notes: Pair parryable attacks with readable windups, audio cues, or optional indicators.
Offer timing-window assists or alternate defensive options if parry is required for progression.

implementation_notes.unity: Model startup, active, recovery, and cancel windows as data, then drive hitboxes, animation events, and damage application from one combat state source. For Parry, validate this path with parameters such as parry_startup_ms and parry_active_window_ms.
implementation_notes.godot: Use Area2D or Area3D hit volumes with frame-window state on the actor; avoid placing damage authority inside animation-only callbacks. For Parry, validate this path with parameters such as parry_startup_ms and parry_active_window_ms.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement the action as a Gameplay Ability or Actor Component with montage notifies for windows and authoritative damage on the server when networked. For Parry, validate this path with parameters such as parry_startup_ms and parry_active_window_ms.
implementation_notes.web: Update combat windows in the fixed simulation step, evaluate hurtbox overlap from cached transforms, and emit presentation events separately from damage state. For Parry, validate this path with parameters such as parry_startup_ms and parry_active_window_ms.

example_games.title: Sekiro: Shadows Die Twice
example_games.note: Uses timed deflection as a central combat skill.

example_games.title: Street Fighter III
example_games.note: Uses parry timing as a high-skill defensive option.
